config.exam_name: 34 Object Level Security or Permissions in Unity Catalog

question.line: What is the primary purpose of Object-Level Security in Unity Catalog?

options.A: To automatically optimize Delta tables for performance
options.B: To encrypt all data stored in Unity Catalog
options.C: To control who can view, access, modify, and manage individual Unity Catalog objects
options.D: To create catalogs and schemas automatically

question.line: Which of the following correctly represents the Unity Catalog object hierarchy?

options.B: Metastore → Catalog → Schema → Tables, Views, Volumes, Functions, and Models

question.line: What happens when a privilege is granted on a parent object, such as a Catalog?

options.A: Permissions apply only to the Catalog itself.
options.B: Child objects automatically inherit the permission.
options.C: All existing permissions on child objects are removed.
options.D: Only Metastore Administrators receive the permission.
options.E: The permission applies only after a manual refresh.

question.line: When is the top-down permission model most appropriate?

options.A: When granting access to only one specific table
options.B: When users need broad access to an entire Catalog or Schema through inherited permissions
options.C: When hiding catalogs from all users
options.E: When assigning permissions to Spark clusters

question.line: Which combination of privileges is required for a user to successfully query a Unity Catalog table?

options.D: USE CATALOG, USE SCHEMA, and SELECT

question.line: What does the BROWSE privilege allow a user to do?

options.A: Read data from tables and views
options.C: Discover catalogs, view metadata, and see lineage without accessing the data itself

question.line: Which privilege allows a user to read files stored in a Unity Catalog Volume?
Which statement about ALL PRIVILEGES is correct?

options.A: It includes the ability to change object ownership.
options.B: It includes the MANAGE privilege automatically.
options.C: It grants nearly every operational permission but does not include managing permissions or ownership.
options.D: It is equivalent to Metastore Administrator privileges.

question.line: What additional capabilities does the MANAGE privilege provide?

options.C: Renaming objects, dropping objects, and changing permissions

question.line: Which approach is recommended for repeatable, automated permission management in Unity Catalog?

options.B: Using only the Catalog Explorer UI
options.C: Using SQL GRANT and REVOKE statements
options.D: Restarting the Metastore after each permission change
options.E: Assigning permissions directly to individual users instead of groups
